_note: Tiered escalation audit. Tier 1 (free army + mimo) broad first pass per surface -> Tier 2 (agy) gate/consolidate -> Tier 3 (sonnet) deep verify against the raw -> Tier 4 (opus) verdict + GO/NO-GO. Every task writes ONLY its findings file under .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/ and edits no other file. Tier 1 is expected to be partial/flaky (free models); sonnet is the reliable backstop.

tasks.doing: Broad first-pass audit of the pigeon source under src/pigeon/ (especially coordinate/routing.py and coordinate/router.py). List anything that looks like a bug, a risky edge case, or a missing test. Write a plain bulleted list to .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/t1-code.md. Do NOT edit any other file. Keep it short and concrete.

Broad first-pass audit of docs/benchmarks/report.md against docs/benchmarks/results/*.json. Spot-check that the numbers in the prose match the JSON data and that no claim looks overstated. Write findings to .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/t1-benchmark.md. Edit no other file.

Broad first-pass audit of docs/benchmarks/manuscript/paper.md. Flag any claim that seems unsupported, any broken path in the reproducibility appendix, and any missing limitation. Write findings to .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/t1-manuscript.md. Edit no other file.

Broad first-pass audit of README.md and the launch/ folder (announcement.md, show-hn.md, social-thread.md, taglines.md). Flag any overclaim, inconsistency between pieces, or dead link. Write findings to .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/t1-materials.md. Edit no other file.

Broad opsec sweep across all files including the untracked launch/ folder. Look for any real personal name, personal email, provider domain, or /home/<user> local path. Confirm only the SlartiLabs alias appears. Write findings to .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/t1-opsec.md. Edit no other file.

You are the GATE (pure read + write, run no shell commands). Read the five tier-1 findings files in .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/ (t1-*.md). Consolidate them: drop duplicates and vague noise, keep every concrete candidate defect, and for each note which surface and how severe it looks. Where a tier-1 file is missing or empty (a free model may have flaked), say so and name that surface as needing a fresh look. Write the consolidated candidate list to .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/gate.md. Edit no other file.

You are the deep verifier. Read .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/gate.md. For EACH candidate defect, open the actual file and confirm or refute it against the raw (do not trust the tier-1/gate claims). Also independently re-audit any surface the gate flagged as under-covered (code, benchmark vs results JSONs, manuscript, materials, opsec) so free-tier flakiness cannot hide a real issue. Write CONFIRMED findings only (file:line, defect, failing scenario, severity blocker/should/nice), plus a short 'refuted' list, to .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/verified.md. Edit no other file.

You are the audit lead. Read .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/verified.md. Re-verify the most severe items against the actual files yourself. Produce the final report at .pigeon/coordinate/reviews/audit2/VERDICT.md: BLOCKERS / SHOULD-FIX / NICE-TO-HAVE (each with file:line and the concrete fix), a 'dropped/cleared' section, and an explicit GO or NO-GO for launch with a one-paragraph rationale. Edit no file except VERDICT.md.
